feedback.details: Diagram syntax is valid. C4Context block structure is correct. UpdateLayoutConfig (c1-context.md:52) is properly placed at the end. All arrows use valid syntax (->, <->). No parsing errors detected.

Major structural flaw: Contains 7 Container_Ext elements when only external dependencies should be present. OpenAPI_Spec (c1-context.md:33) and Anthropic_Spec (c1-context.md:34) are API specifications/standards, not external systems. Batch_Support (c1-context.md:39) is an internal capability, not an external dependency. This violates the requirement for exactly one container per external dependency.

Missing the mandatory table mapping each diagram element (Developer, Operations, llm-switch, each Container_Ext) to its purpose and PRD section. Only a relationship table exists (c1-context.md:57-67), not an element mapping table. Hallucinated elements (OpenAPI_Spec, Anthropic_Spec, Batch_Support) cannot be traced to actual PRD Integrations requirements.

Missing the required table mapping each diagram element to its purpose and PRD section. Word count exceeds 200 (satisfied). Dedicated paragraphs for dependencies exist (c1-context.md:71-83), but the mandatory element-to-PRD mapping table is absent, failing this criterion.

Semantic errors in relationship definitions: llm-switch -> OpenAPI_Spec (c1-context.md:44) claims 'Implements OpenAPI specification' - you implement standards, you do not have runtime relationships with them. Same invalid pattern for Anthropic_Spec (c1-context.md:45) and Batch_Support (c1-context.md:50). Table lists protocol as 'N/A' for these (c1-context.md:61-62, 67), which is incorrect - they are not valid relationships.

Automatic failure criterion triggered. Three hallucinated external dependencies not in PRD Integrations: (1) OpenAPI Specification is a standard, not an external container (c1-context.md:33); (2) Anthropic Message API is a protocol specification, not a system (c1-context.md:34); (3) Batch Processing is an internal capability/feature, not an external dependency (c1-context.md:39). Each extra container constitutes failure.

Correctly implemented. llm-switch is the sole System element (c1-context.md:31). All external systems use Container_Ext (c1-context.md:33-39), correctly placing them outside the system boundary. No external dependencies drawn inside boundary.

Severe violations of <=25 character label rule: (1) Person_Ext description 'Integrates and uses llm-switch via OpenAPI/Anthropic-compatible APIs [PRD 5]' is 83 characters (c1-context.md:28); (2) System description 'Intelligent LLM Proxy System providing dynamic model selection [PRD 1]' is 66 characters (c1-context.md:31); (3) Multiple Container_Ext descriptions exceed 50 characters. System ID 'llm-switch' uses lowercase with hyphen instead of PascalCase (c1-context.md:31), violating C4 naming conventions.

MD013 violations: Line 10 is 144 characters (>120 limit). Line 73 is 158 characters. Line 75 is 187 characters. Frontmatter present with sprint_number and sprint_name (c1-context.md:1-4). No inline HTML detected. No tabs detected. Fails MD013 line length requirement.

overall_summary: This architecture document is fundamentally broken and cannot proceed to production. It fails the automatic failure criterion for hallucination prevention by including three elements that are not external dependencies: OpenAPI Specification and Anthropic Message API are API standards/protocols, not systems; Batch Processing is an internal capability. The C4 naming violations are severe with labels up to 83 characters when the limit is 25. The mandatory element-to-PRD mapping table is completely absent. Line lengths violate MD013 throughout. The only passing criteria are Mermaid syntax validity and boundary integrity. This represents a fundamental misunderstanding of C4 notation (confusing specifications with systems) and basic formatting requirements.
